College of Arts and Humanities 2101 Woods Hall Phone: 301-405-6877 wgss@umd.edu http://wgss.umd.edu

Department Chair: Neda Atanasoski, Ph.D.

See Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies Department (https:// academiccatalog.umd.edu/undergraduate/colleges-schools/arts- humanities/women-gender-sexuality-studies/#facultytext) for faculty roster.

The LGBTQ minor in the Department of Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies is a 15 credit program which provides students an opportunity for interdisciplinary study of the lives, experiences, identities, and representations of LGBT people. This course of study provides solid grounding in the major concepts and methods that deﬁne studies of sex, gender, and gender identity today. Any student in good standing in the university may enroll in the LGBTQ Studies minor. In consultation with the Department of Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies academic advisor, students will design a minor program that complements their major ﬁeld of study.

For additional information, or to initiate the process of declaring the minor, please contact the undergraduate advisor at wgss@umd.edu or 301-405-6827. Students are advised to meet with the department advisor by the time they have reached 90 credits, if not before.
